tools.description: Create a new GitHub issue for tracking bugs, feature requests, or tasks. Use this for actionable work items that need assignment, labeling, and status tracking. For reports, announcements, or status updates that don't require task tracking, use create_discussion instead. CONSTRAINTS: Maximum 1 issue(s) can be created. Labels [test-reliability] will be automatically added.

body.description: Detailed issue description in Markdown. Do NOT repeat the title as a heading since it already appears as the issue's h1. Include context, reproduction steps, or acceptance criteria as appropriate.

labels.description: Labels to categorize the issue (e.g., 'bug', 'enhancement'). Labels must exist in the repository.

parent.description: Parent issue number for creating sub-issues. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 42 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/42). Can also be a temporary_id (e.g., 'aw_abc123', 'aw_Test123') from a previously created issue in the same workflow run.

temporary_id.description: Unique temporary identifier for referencing this issue before it's created. Format: 'aw_' followed by 3 to 8 alphanumeric characters (e.g., 'aw_abc1', 'aw_Test123'). Use '#aw_ID' in body text to reference other issues by their temporary_id; these are replaced with actual issue numbers after creation.

title.description: Concise issue title summarizing the bug, feature, or task. The title appears as the main heading, so keep it brief and descriptive.

tools.name: create_issue

tools.description: Report that a tool or capability needed to complete the task is not available, or share any information you deem important about missing functionality or limitations. Use this when you cannot accomplish what was requested because the required functionality is missing or access is restricted.

alternatives.description: Any workarounds, manual steps, or alternative approaches the user could take (max 256 characters).

reason.description: Explanation of why this tool is needed or what information you want to share about the limitation (max 256 characters).

tool.description: Optional: Name or description of the missing tool or capability (max 128 characters). Be specific about what functionality is needed.

tools.name: missing_tool

tools.description: Log a transparency message when no significant actions are needed. Use this to confirm workflow completion and provide visibility when analysis is complete but no changes or outputs are required (e.g., 'No issues found', 'All checks passed'). This ensures the workflow produces human-visible output even when no other actions are taken.

message.description: Status or completion message to log. Should explain what was analyzed and the outcome (e.g., 'Code review complete - no issues found', 'Analysis complete - all tests passing').

tools.name: noop

tools.description: Report that data or information needed to complete the task is not available. Use this when you cannot accomplish what was requested because required data, context, or information is missing.

alternatives.description: Any workarounds, manual steps, or alternative approaches the user could take (max 256 characters).

context.description: Additional context about the missing data or where it should come from (max 256 characters).

data_type.description: Type or description of the missing data or information (max 128 characters). Be specific about what data is needed.

reason.description: Explanation of why this data is needed to complete the task (max 256 characters).

tools.name: missing_data

tools.description: Get details for a commit from a GitHub repository

include_diff.description: Whether to include file diffs and stats in the response. Default is true.

perPage.description: Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)

sha.description: Commit SHA, branch name, or tag name

tools.name: get_commit

tools.description: Get the contents of a file or directory from a GitHub repository

ref.description: Accepts optional git refs such as `refs/tags/{tag}`, `refs/heads/{branch}` or `refs/pull/{pr_number}/head`

sha.description: Accepts optional commit SHA. If specified, it will be used instead of ref

tools.name: get_file_contents

annotations.title: Get a specific label from a repository.

tools.description: Get a specific label from a repository.
tools.name: get_label

tools.description: Get the latest release in a GitHub repository
tools.name: get_latest_release

tools.description: Get details of the authenticated GitHub user. Use this when a request is about the user's own profile for GitHub. Or when information is missing to build other tool calls.
tools.name: get_me

annotations.title: Get a release by tag name

tools.description: Get a specific release by its tag name in a GitHub repository
tools.name: get_release_by_tag

tools.description: Get details about a specific git tag in a GitHub repository
tools.name: get_tag

tools.description: Get member usernames of a specific team in an organization. Limited to organizations accessible with current credentials

org.description: Organization login (owner) that contains the team.

tools.name: get_team_members

tools.description: Get details of the teams the user is a member of. Limited to organizations accessible with current credentials

user.description: Username to get teams for. If not provided, uses the authenticated user.

tools.name: get_teams

tools.description: Get information about a specific issue in a GitHub repository.

method.description:
The read operation to perform on a single issue.
Options are:
1. get - Get details of a specific issue.
2. get_comments - Get issue comments.
3. get_sub_issues - Get sub-issues of the issue.
4. get_labels - Get labels assigned to the issue.

perPage.description: Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)

tools.name: issue_read

tools.description: List branches in a GitHub repository

perPage.description: Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)

tools.name: list_branches

tools.description: Get list of commits of a branch in a GitHub repository. Returns at least 30 results per page by default, but can return more if specified using the perPage parameter (up to 100).

author.description: Author username or email address to filter commits by

perPage.description: Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)

sha.description: Commit SHA, branch or tag name to list commits of. If not provided, uses the default branch of the repository. If a commit SHA is provided, will list commits up to that SHA.

tools.name: list_commits

tools.description: List supported issue types for repository owner (organization).

owner.description: The organization owner of the repository

tools.name: list_issue_types

tools.description: List issues in a GitHub repository. For pagination, use the 'endCursor' from the previous response's 'pageInfo' in the 'after' parameter.

after.description: Cursor for pagination. Use the endCursor from the previous page's PageInfo for GraphQL APIs.

direction.description: Order direction. If provided, the 'orderBy' also needs to be provided.

orderBy.description: Order issues by field. If provided, the 'direction' also needs to be provided.

perPage.description: Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)

state.description: Filter by state, by default both open and closed issues are returned when not provided

tools.name: list_issues

tools.description: List pull requests in a GitHub repository. If the user specifies an author, then DO NOT use this tool and use the search_pull_requests tool instead.

head.description: Filter by head user/org and branch

perPage.description: Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)

tools.name: list_pull_requests

tools.description: List releases in a GitHub repository

perPage.description: Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)

tools.name: list_releases

tools.description: List git tags in a GitHub repository

perPage.description: Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)

tools.name: list_tags

annotations.title: Get details for a single pull request

tools.description: Get information on a specific pull request in GitHub repository.

method.description:
Action to specify what pull request data needs to be retrieved from GitHub. 
Possible options: 
 1. get - Get details of a specific pull request.
 2. get_diff - Get the diff of a pull request.
 3. get_status - Get status of a head commit in a pull request. This reflects status of builds and checks.
 4. get_files - Get the list of files changed in a pull request. Use with pagination parameters to control the number of results returned.
 5. get_review_comments - Get review threads on a pull request. Each thread contains logically grouped review comments made on the same code location during pull request reviews. Returns threads with metadata (isResolved, isOutdated, isCollapsed) and their associated comments. Use cursor-based pagination (perPage, after) to control results.
 6. get_reviews - Get the reviews on a pull request. When asked for review comments, use get_review_comments method.
 7. get_comments - Get comments on a pull request. Use this if user doesn't specifically want review comments. Use with pagination parameters to control the number of results returned.

perPage.description: Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)

tools.name: pull_request_read

tools.description: Fast and precise code search across ALL GitHub repositories using GitHub's native search engine. Best for finding exact symbols, functions, classes, or specific code patterns.

perPage.description: Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)

query.description: Search query using GitHub's powerful code search syntax. Examples: 'content:Skill language:Java org:github', 'NOT is:archived language:Python OR language:go', 'repo:github/github-mcp-server'. Supports exact matching, language filters, path filters, and more.

tools.name: search_code

tools.description: Search for issues in GitHub repositories using issues search syntax already scoped to is:issue

owner.description: Optional repository owner. If provided with repo, only issues for this repository are listed.

perPage.description: Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)

query.description: Search query using GitHub issues search syntax

repo.description: Optional repository name. If provided with owner, only issues for this repository are listed.

sort.description: Sort field by number of matches of categories, defaults to best match

tools.name: search_issues

tools.description: Search for pull requests in GitHub repositories using issues search syntax already scoped to is:pr

owner.description: Optional repository owner. If provided with repo, only pull requests for this repository are listed.

perPage.description: Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)

query.description: Search query using GitHub pull request search syntax

repo.description: Optional repository name. If provided with owner, only pull requests for this repository are listed.

sort.description: Sort field by number of matches of categories, defaults to best match

tools.name: search_pull_requests

tools.description: Find GitHub repositories by name, description, readme, topics, or other metadata. Perfect for discovering projects, finding examples, or locating specific repositories across GitHub.

minimal_output.description: Return minimal repository information (default: true). When false, returns full GitHub API repository objects.

perPage.description: Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)

query.description: Repository search query. Examples: 'machine learning in:name stars:>1000 language:python', 'topic:react', 'user:facebook'. Supports advanced search syntax for precise filtering.

sort.description: Sort repositories by field, defaults to best match

tools.name: search_repositories

### Packages impacted by this PR
@azure/monitor-opentelemetry

### Issues associated with this PR


### Describe the problem that is addressed by this PR
This pull request updates the Azure Monitor OpenTelemetry SDK to replace
the custom `AzureFunctionsHook` with the officially supported
`@azure/functions-opentelemetry-instrumentation` package for Azure
Functions context propagation. This change simplifies the codebase,
removes custom module loading logic, and ensures the latest Azure
Functions instrumentation features are available. Related tests have
been updated to reflect the new approach.

Key changes include:

**Azure Functions Instrumentation Modernization:**
* Replaced the custom `AzureFunctionsHook` implementation with the
`@azure/functions-opentelemetry-instrumentation` package, which is now
always included as an instrumentation. This package is maintained by the
Azure Functions team and adds capabilities such as log forwarding and
support for `WorkerOpenTelemetryEnabled`.
(`sdk/monitor/monitor-opentelemetry/CHANGELOG.md`,
`sdk/monitor/monitor-opentelemetry/package.json`,
`sdk/monitor/monitor-opentelemetry/src/traces/handler.ts`,
[[1]](diffhunk://#diff-[REDACTED]R9)
[[2]](diffhunk://#diff-[REDACTED]R76)
[[3]](diffhunk://#diff-[REDACTED]L26-R26)
[[4]](diffhunk://#diff-[REDACTED]L40)
[[5]](diffhunk://#diff-[REDACTED]L71)
[[6]](diffhunk://#diff-[REDACTED]L95)
[[7]](diffhunk://#diff-[REDACTED]R153)

**Codebase Simplification and Cleanup:**
* Removed the custom Azure Functions hook implementation and associated
module loader utilities, cleaning up both CommonJS and ESM-specific
code. (`sdk/monitor/monitor-opentelemetry/src/traces/azureFnHook.ts`,
`sdk/monitor/monitor-opentelemetry/src/shared/module.ts`,
`sdk/monitor/monitor-opentelemetry/src/shared/module-cjs.cts`,
[[1]](diffhunk://#diff-[REDACTED]L1-L96)
[[2]](diffhunk://#diff-[REDACTED]L4-L26)
[[3]](diffhunk://#diff-[REDACTED]L8-L20)

**Test Updates:**
* Updated and simplified unit tests to verify that
`AzureFunctionsInstrumentation` is always registered, and removed tests
specific to the old custom hook logic.
(`sdk/monitor/monitor-opentelemetry/test/internal/unit/traces/azureFnHook.test.ts`,
`sdk/monitor/monitor-opentelemetry/test/internal/unit/traces/traceHandler.test.ts`,
[[1]](diffhunk://#diff-[REDACTED]L4-R12)
[[2]](diffhunk://#diff-[REDACTED]L31)
[[3]](diffhunk://#diff-[REDACTED]L43-R46)
[[4]](diffhunk://#diff-[REDACTED]L371-R372)

**Minor Code Comments:**
* Improved a comment in `QuickpulseSender` to clarify test access
patterns.
(`sdk/monitor/monitor-opentelemetry/src/metrics/quickpulse/export/sender.ts`,
[sdk/monitor/monitor-opentelemetry/src/metrics/quickpulse/export/sender.tsL43-R43](diffhunk://#diff-[REDACTED]L43-R43))

### Packages impacted by this PR
@azure/monitor-opentelemetry-exporter

### Describe the problem that is addressed by this PR
This pull request addresses a bug in the retry logic for throttled
telemetry in the OpenTelemetry exporter. The main improvement ensures
that when multiple `Retry-After` headers are received, the exporter uses
the longest delay, preventing premature retries and improving envelope
delivery reliability.

Bug Fixes:

* Updated the retry timer logic in `baseSender.ts` to reschedule when a
new `Retry-After` value is longer than the existing timer, ensuring
envelopes are sent at the latest required time.
* Modified the corresponding unit test in `baseSender.spec.ts` to verify
that the retry timer is rescheduled for longer delays, aligning test
behavior with the new logic.
* Added a changelog entry documenting the bug fix regarding multiple
`Retry-After` headers and the use of the longest delay.

### Issues associated with this PR

### Describe the problem that is addressed by this PR

### Packages impacted by this PR
@azure/monitor-opentelemetry-exporter

### Describe the problem that is addressed by this PR
This pull request updates the handling of custom dimension property size
limits in the OpenTelemetry exporter, introducing a higher limit for Gen
AI-related properties and updating the truncation logic and tests
accordingly. The changes ensure Gen AI properties are truncated at
256KB, while other properties remain at the 64KB limit.

**Custom dimension truncation logic:**

* Added `MaxPropertyLengths.EIGHTEEN_BIT` (256KB) to `types.ts` for the
new Gen AI property size limit.
* Updated `truncateCustomDimensions` in `common.ts` to apply a 256KB
limit for Gen AI keys and retain the 64KB limit for other properties;
also revised debug messaging.
[[1]](diffhunk://#diff-[REDACTED]L306-R315)
[[2]](diffhunk://#diff-[REDACTED]L323-R326)
[[3]](diffhunk://#diff-[REDACTED]L332-R335)

**Test updates:**

* Modified tests in `customDimensionsLimits.spec.ts` to verify the new
256KB limit for Gen AI keys and ensure correct truncation behavior for
both exempt (Gen AI) and non-exempt keys.

[[2]](diffhunk://#diff-[REDACTED]L105-R113)
[[3]](diffhunk://#diff-[REDACTED]R178-R181)

## Description

Bumps `fast-xml-parser` from `^5.3.7` to `^5.5.6` in
`sdk/core/core-xml`.

Fixes #37665

## Changes

- `sdk/core/core-xml/package.json`: updated `fast-xml-parser` version
constraint
- `sdk/core/core-xml/CHANGELOG.md`: added unreleased changelog entry
- `pnpm-lock.yaml`: updated lockfile to resolve `fast-xml-parser@5.5.6`
(also picks up `fast-xml-builder@1.1.4` and transitive dep
`path-expression-matcher@1.1.3`)

## Backwards Compatibility

The public API used by `core-xml` is fully preserved across 5.3.7 →
5.5.6:
- `XMLBuilder`, `XMLParser`, `XMLValidator` exports are unchanged
- All options used (`attributesGroupName`, `textNodeName`,
`parseTagValue`, `stopNodes`, etc.) still exist
- Changes between versions are purely additive: new options, security
fixes, and bug fixes

### Packages impacted by this PR

None (infrastructure/tooling only)

### Issues associated with this PR

Adds `.github/lsp.json` so GitHub Copilot CLI can use
`typescript-language-server` for code intelligence in this repo.

### Describe the problem that is addressed by this PR

Copilot CLI supports [repository-level LSP
configuration](https://github.com/github/copilot-cli?tab=readme-ov-file#configuring-lsp-servers)
to enable go-to-definition, hover, and diagnostics. This repo had no
such configuration.

### What are the possible designs available to address the problem? If
there are more than one possible design, why was the one in this PR
chosen?

Single file at `.github/lsp.json` per the Copilot CLI docs. Maps `.ts`,
`.tsx`, `.mts`, and `.cts` to `typescript-language-server --stdio`.

### Are there test cases added in this PR? _(If not, why?)_

No — this is a static JSON configuration file consumed by external
tooling.

None

### Packages impacted by this PR

`eng/tools/analyze-deps`, `eng/tools/generate-doc`,
`eng/tools/js-sdk-release-tools`

### Issues associated with this PR


### Describe the problem that is addressed by this PR

Runs `npm audit fix` across all npm-managed packages under `eng/tools`
to resolve known dependency vulnerabilities.

**Fixed (lock file updates only):**
- `analyze-deps`: `tar` 7.5.4 → 7.5.11 (path traversal via
hardlink/symlink)
- `generate-doc`: `markdown-it` 14.1.0 → 14.1.1 (ReDoS), `minimatch`
9.0.5 → 9.0.9 (ReDoS)
- `js-sdk-release-tools`: `flatted` 3.3.4 → 3.4.1 (unbounded recursion
DoS), `tar` 7.5.9 → 7.5.11 (path traversal)

**No vulnerabilities found:** `eng-package-utils`, `versioning`

**Unfixable without breaking change:** `ajv` ReDoS in
`js-sdk-release-tools` — transitive via `@typespec/compiler`, fix
requires upstream update.

### What are the possible designs available to address the problem? If
there are more than one possible design, why was the one in this PR
chosen?

`npm audit fix` (non-breaking semver-compatible updates) vs `npm audit
fix --force` (allows breaking changes). Used non-force to avoid
downgrading `@azure-tools/js-sdk-release-tools` from 2.16.4 to 2.7.23.

### Are there test cases added in this PR? _(If not, why?)_

No — changes are limited to `package-lock.json` files (dependency
version bumps within existing semver ranges).

### Packages impacted by this PR

ci-runner

### Describe the problem that is addressed by this PR

Add a new ci-runner action that verifies packages with already-published
versions have no source modifications since the release tag. Fails if a
published version has modified files, indicating the version needs to be
bumped.

### What are the possible designs available to address the problem? If
there are more than one possible design, why was the one in this PR
chosen?

Currently we solve this by automatically opening version bump PRs after
release. However, these PRs do not always get merged in a timely fashion
and generate considerable noise.

### Are there test cases added in this PR? _(If not, why?)_

Yes

**Background**
All management SDKs are auto generated and their PR reviewings are
mainly rule-based. The internal implementation seems un-necessary and
direct suggested changes are wrong guidances. So we build the rule-based
review process in agentica workflow to highlight checkpoints mgmt
reviewers care and provide correct suggestions when issue happens.

**How to trigger?**
Adding the label `mgmt-review-needed` would automatcially trigger our
rule-based reviews.

**Detail**
Add management SDK review agent and agentic workflow that triggers when
the 'mgmt-review-needed' label is added to a PR. The reviews for public
API design issues including breaking changes, naming conventions and API
consistency and tool validation rules to detect any tool issues during
preparing AutoPRs.

This is the first version and we would continue improving its quality
https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-js/issues/37631.

**Testing PRs**
- https://github.com/MaryGao/azure-sdk-for-js/pull/17
- https://github.com/MaryGao/azure-sdk-for-js/pull/18
- https://github.com/MaryGao/azure-sdk-for-js/pull/19

Co-authored-by: kazrael2119 <98569699+kazrael2119@users.noreply.github.com>","author":{"name":"Azure SDK Bot","email":"53356347+azure-sdk@users.noreply.github.com","date":"2026-03-17T16:07:15Z"},"committer":{"name":"GitHub","email":"noreply@github.com","date":"2026-03-17T16:07:15Z"}},"author":{"login":"azure-sdk","id":53356347,"profile_url":"https://github.com/azure-sdk","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/53356347?v=4"},"committer":{"login":"web-flow","id":19864447,"profile_url":"https://github.com/web-flow","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/19864447?v=4"}},{"sha":"[REDACTED]","html_url":"https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-js/commit/[REDACTED]","commit":{"message":"[search] Add agent skill for search-documents package (#37484)

This adds an [agent skill](https://agentskills.io/specification) for the
search-documents package - encoding the package-specific tribal
knowledge that an AI agent needs when working on this library.

### Motivation

Working on `@azure/search-documents` has a lot of package-specific
gotchas: the custom pagination implementation, the
`additionalProperties` unwrapping pattern, the type conversion layer in
`serviceUtils.ts`, pipe-delimited semantic encoding, the known skills
whitelist that silently drops unknown types, etc. This skill encodes
that knowledge so an agent can load it on demand and avoid the usual
iteration cycles.

### What's here

**Registry skill** (`.github/skills/find-package-skill/SKILL.md`)
- Discovered automatically by agents from the repo root
- Routes to the correct package-specific skill based on what package is
being modified
- Instructs the agent to read the SKILL.md and any `references/` files

**Package skill**
(`sdk/search/search-documents/.github/skills/search-documents/`)
- `SKILL.md` - the main skill covering architecture, regeneration, the
four clients, custom pagination, data flow patterns
(additionalProperties unwrapping, serialization sandwich, field
visibility inversion), wire format encodings, type mappings, and common
pitfalls
- `references/customization.md` - the 3-way merge algorithm and common
post-regeneration scenarios
- `references/type-mapping.md` - property name changes and conversion
function inventory

### Conventions

I wanted to set up a pattern that other packages could follow:
- **Encapsulation**: skill lives alongside the package it describes
(`sdk/<service>/<package>/.github/skills/<name>/`)
- **Registry**: `find-package-skill` at the repo root provides the entry
point - package skills don't need to be in a global scan path
- **Scope**: only package-specific tribal knowledge, not generic repo or
SDK guidance (build commands, test framework, etc. belong in
`AGENTS.md`)
- **References**: deeper detail in `references/` directory for

stable releases and regenerates the lock file.

### Packages impacted by this PR

### Describe the problem that is addressed by this PR

TypeSpec emitter dependencies in `eng/emitter-package.json` were pinned
to outdated versions. This PR bumps them to latest stable and
regenerates the lock file.

**`eng/emitter-package.json` version changes:**
- `@typespec/{events,rest,streams,versioning}`: `0.79.0` → `0.80.0`
- `@typespec/{openapi,openapi3}`: `1.9.0` → `1.10.0`
- `@typespec/http`: `1.9.1` → `1.10.0`
- `@azure-tools/typespec-ts`: `0.50.4` → `0.51.0`
-
`@azure-tools/typespec-{azure-core,autorest,azure-resource-manager,azure-rulesets}`:
`0.65.0` → `0.66.0`
- `@azure-tools/typespec-client-generator-core`: `0.65.4` → `0.66.2`
- `@azure-tools/typespec-liftr-base`: `0.12.0` → `0.13.0`

**`.scripts/automation_generate.sh`:**
- `@autorest/typescript`: `^6.0.63` → `^6.0.64`

**`eng/emitter-package-lock.json`:** Regenerated via `tsp-client
generate-lock-file`.

### Packages impacted by this PR
@azure/monitor-opentelemetry-exporter

### Issues associated with this PR

https://github.com/microsoft/ApplicationInsights-node.js/issues/317#issuecomment-3336028029

### Describe the problem that is addressed by this PR
This pull request introduces improvements to the Azure Monitor
OpenTelemetry Exporter, focusing on handling throttled telemetry and
respecting backend retry guidance. The exporter now persists throttled
telemetry for retry, and uses the `Retry-After` header to schedule
retries more accurately. Several tests and utility functions were added
to ensure robust handling of retry scenarios.

**Telemetry retry and persistence enhancements:**

* Throttled telemetry (HTTP 429/439 responses) is now persisted to disk
for retry instead of being silently dropped, improving reliability for
transient backend throttling.
[[1]](diffhunk://#diff-[REDACTED]R3-R9)
[[2]](diffhunk://#diff-[REDACTED]L157-R152)
* The exporter now respects the `Retry-After` header from the backend
when scheduling retries for both throttled and successful telemetry,
using a new `scheduleRetryTimer` method.
[[1]](diffhunk://#diff-[REDACTED]L130-R137)
[[2]](diffhunk://#diff-[REDACTED]R397-R407)
[[3]](diffhunk://#diff-[REDACTED]L76-R81)

**Utility and type improvements:**

* Added the `parseRetryAfterHeader` utility function to robustly parse
both delay-seconds and HTTP-date formats from the `Retry-After` header.
* Updated the `SenderResult` type to include an optional `retryAfterMs`
property, enabling downstream logic to use backend-provided retry
intervals.

**Testing enhancements:**

* Added and extended tests to verify envelope persistence and retry
scheduling on throttled responses, as well as correct parsing and usage
of the `Retry-After` header.
[[1]](diffhunk://#diff-[REDACTED]R654-R705)
[[2]](diffhunk://#diff-[REDACTED]R1-R55)

regression in some playback tests where double slash is included in

@azure/cosmos

already exhausted were not removed from the continuation token, leading
to an inflated token size. This fix ensures exhausted ranges are
properly cleaned up.

Upgrades `@azure-tools/typespec-ts` to `0.50.4` and
`@autorest/typescript` to `^6.0.63`, then regenerates the emitter lock
file.

### Changes

- **`eng/emitter-package.json`**: `@azure-tools/typespec-ts` `0.50.3` →
`0.50.4`
- **`eng/emitter-package-lock.json`**: Regenerated via `tsp-client
generate-lock-file`; picks up `@azure-tools/rlc-common@0.50.4` as
updated transitive dep
- **`.scripts/automation_generate.sh`**: `@autorest/typescript`
`^6.0.62` → `^6.0.63`

> Other packages (`@azure-tools/typespec-azure-core`, `@typespec/rest`,
etc.) remain on the 0.65.x/0.79.x generation — `typespec-ts@0.50.4` peer
deps constrain them; upgrading to 0.66.x/0.80.x would break dependency
resolution.

### Packages impacted by this PR

`eng/emitter-package.json`, `.scripts/automation_generate.sh`

### Issues associated with this PR


### Describe the problem that is addressed by this PR

TypeSpec emitter packages were outdated. This bumps the JS emitter
(`@azure-tools/typespec-ts`) and autorest codegen tool to their latest
compatible versions.

### What are the possible designs available to address the problem? If
there are more than one possible design, why was the one in this PR
chosen?

N/A — straightforward version bump constrained by peer dependency
compatibility.

### Are there test cases added in this PR? _(If not, why?)_

No — configuration-only change; lock file is regenerated from the
updated `emitter-package.json`.

### Provide a list of related PRs _(if any)_

N/A

### Command used to generate this PR:**_(Applicable only to SDK release
request PRs)_

```bash
tsp-client generate-lock-file --emitter-package-json-path ./eng/emitter-package.json
```

Updates TypeSpec emitter dependencies to the latest compatible versions
and regenerates the lock file.

### Packages impacted by this PR
`eng/emitter-package.json`, `eng/emitter-package-lock.json`,
`.scripts/automation_generate.sh`

### Issues associated with this PR


### Describe the problem that is addressed by this PR
Emitter dependencies were pinned to outdated versions. Upgrades are
limited by peer dependency constraints in
`@azure-tools/typespec-ts@0.50.3` (requires
`@azure-tools/typespec-azure-core@^0.65.0`, `@typespec/rest@^0.79.0`,
etc.), so only compatible packages were bumped:

- `@azure-tools/typespec-ts`: `0.50.2` → `0.50.3`
- `@typespec/compiler`: `1.9.0` → `1.10.0`
- `@autorest/typescript` range in `automation_generate.sh`: `^6.0.61` →
`^6.0.62`

Lock file regenerated via `tsp-client generate-lock-file`.

### What are the possible designs available to address the problem? If
there are more than one possible design, why was the one in this PR
chosen?
No design choice — straightforward version bump to latest
semver-compatible releases.

### Are there test cases added in this PR? _(If not, why?)_
No — dependency version updates to build tooling; no functional code
changed.

N/A

### Packages impacted by this PR
@azure/monitor-opentelemetry-exporter

### Describe the problem that is addressed by this PR
This pull request refactors how the 64KB size limit for custom
dimensions is managed, especially for Gen AI scenarios. Instead of using
the environment variable `AZURE_MONITOR_DISABLE_CUSTOM_DIMENSIONS_LIMIT`
to disable truncation globally, it now exempts a specific set of Gen AI
property keys from truncation. The tests have been updated accordingly
to reflect this more targeted exemption.

**Custom Dimensions Truncation Policy:**

- Replaces the environment variable-based opt-out
(`ENV_AZURE_MONITOR_DISABLE_CUSTOM_DIMENSIONS_LIMIT`) with a hardcoded
set of exempt property keys (`CUSTOM_DIMENSIONS_EXEMPT_KEYS`) that are
allowed to exceed the 64KB limit, specifically for Gen AI payloads.
[[1]](diffhunk://#diff-[REDACTED]L145-R157)
[[2]](diffhunk://#diff-[REDACTED]L44-R44)
[[3]](diffhunk://#diff-[REDACTED]L306-R307)
[[4]](diffhunk://#diff-[REDACTED]L324-R323)

**Testing Updates:**

- Removes all test logic and cases related to the environment variable,
and adds/updates tests to ensure only the specified exempt keys are not
truncated, while all others (including non-exempt Gen AI keys) are still
subject to the 64KB limit.
[[1]](diffhunk://#diff-[REDACTED]L5-L24)
[[2]](diffhunk://#diff-[REDACTED]L94-R125)

## Describe the problem that is addressed by this PR

#37546 removed `backcompatTypes.ts` since these types are now generated
from TypeSpec, but we forgot to re-export them from the customization
layer. This meant `KnownEntityCategory`,
`KnownEntityRecognitionSkillLanguage`, and `KnownSentimentSkillLanguage`
were no longer available to consumers of the library.

This PR adds the enum and type alias definitions directly in the `src/`
customization layer (matching the generated code in content and position
to keep future `customization apply` merges clean) and re-exports them
through the public API.

API report now shows all three enums and their associated type aliases

Co-authored-by: Chidozie Ononiwu <chononiw@microsoft.com>","author":{"name":"Azure SDK Bot","email":"53356347+azure-sdk@users.noreply.github.com","date":"2026-03-09T23:02:00Z"},"committer":{"name":"GitHub","email":"noreply@github.com","date":"2026-03-09T23:02:00Z"}},"author":{"login":"azure-sdk","id":53356347,"profile_url":"https://github.com/azure-sdk","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/53356347?v=4"},"committer":{"login":"web-flow","id":19864447,"profile_url":"https://github.com/web-flow","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/19864447?v=4"}},{"sha":"[REDACTED]","html_url":"https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-js/commit/[REDACTED]","commit":{"message":"[Monitor OpenTelemetry] Fix Span Duration Calculations (#37524)

### Packages impacted by this PR
@azure/monitor-opentelemetry

### Describe the problem that is addressed by this PR
This pull request fixes a critical bug in the way span durations are
calculated for standard metrics and performance counters in the
`monitor-opentelemetry` SDK. Previously, only the seconds component of
the duration was used, causing all sub-second requests to be recorded as
0ms. The code now correctly converts the full high-resolution time tuple
to milliseconds using `hrTimeToMilliseconds()`. Unit tests have been
updated to reflect the correct duration format.

Bug fix for duration calculation:

* Updated both `standardMetrics.ts` and `performanceCounters.ts` to use
`hrTimeToMilliseconds()` from `@opentelemetry/core`, ensuring that span
durations are accurately recorded in milliseconds instead of just
seconds. This resolves the issue of sub-second requests being logged as
0ms.
[[1]](diffhunk://#diff-[REDACTED]R24)
[[2]](diffhunk://#diff-[REDACTED]L124-R125)
[[3]](diffhunk://#diff-[REDACTED]R21)
[[4]](diffhunk://#diff-[REDACTED]L200-R201)

Unit test updates:

* Modified all relevant unit tests in `performanceMetrics.test.ts` and
`standardMetrics.test.ts` to use the correct `[seconds, nanoseconds]`
tuple format for span durations, ensuring test accuracy and coverage for
the new duration calculation logic.

calculation in standard metrics and performance counters.

## Description

Adds `BusinessRegistrationDetails` to the `CampaignBrief` model in
`@azure-tools/communication-toll-free-verification`, bumping to
`v1.0.0-beta.2`.

Also brings `privacyPolicyUrl` and `termsAndConditionsUrl` into the
swagger contract — these fields exist in the Number Management backend
domain model but were previously missing from the swagger/SDK, causing a
mismatch.

### Changes

- **swagger/swagger.json**: Add `BusinessRegistrationDetails` definition
with 4 properties; add `privacyPolicyUrl` and `termsAndConditionsUrl` to
`CampaignBrief`
- **src/generated/src/models/index.ts**: Add
`BusinessRegistrationDetails` interface, `BusinessEntityType` and
`BusinessRegistrationType` enum types, and new `CampaignBrief`
properties (autorest-generated)
- **src/generated/src/models/mappers.ts**: Add
`BusinessRegistrationDetails` mapper and new field entries in
`CampaignBrief` mapper (autorest-generated)
- **src/generated/src/tracing.ts**: Bump `packageVersion` to
`1.0.0-beta.2`

### BusinessRegistrationDetails fields

| Field | Type | Description |
|-------|------|-------------|
| `businessRegistrationNumber` | `string` | Registration number (e.g.
EIN, VAT). Max 500 chars. |
| `businessEntityType` | `BusinessEntityType` enum | SOLE_PROPRIETOR,
PRIVATE_PROFIT, PUBLIC_PROFIT, NON_PROFIT, GOVERNMENT |
| `businessRegistrationType` | `BusinessRegistrationType` enum | EIN,
CBN, NEQ, VAT, ACN, ABN, and 12 others |
| `businessRegistrationCountry` | `string` | ISO 3166-1 Alpha-2 country
code |

### Generation

```bash
npx autorest swagger/README.md --force
```

Co-authored-by: kazrael2119 <98569699+kazrael2119@users.noreply.github.com>","author":{"name":"Copilot","email":"198982749+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com","date":"2026-03-09T11:46:26Z"},"committer":{"name":"GitHub","email":"noreply@github.com","date":"2026-03-09T11:46:26Z"}},"author":{"login":"Copilot","id":198982749,"profile_url":"https://github.com/apps/copilot-swe-agent","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/in/1143301?v=4"},"committer":{"login":"web-flow","id":19864447,"profile_url":"https://github.com/web-flow","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/19864447?v=4"}},{"sha":"[REDACTED]","html_url":"https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-js/commit/[REDACTED]","commit":{"message":"[ts-http-runtime] Fix URL construction in urlHelpers.ts (#37302)

There are cases where the `routePath` isn't a real path, but in the form
of `"?restype=service&comp=properties"`. They are supposed to be query
parameters but today are appended to the endpoint directly. This caused
issues when the endpoint already contains query parameters (e.g.,
storage SAS URL).

This PR handles this case and ports code from `@azure/core-client` for appending path and merging query params.


### Packages impacted by this PR
`@typespec/ts-http-runtime`

`@azure/core-xml` declared `fast-xml-parser: ^5.0.7`, allowing versions
4.1.3–5.3.5 affected by two critical CVEs to satisfy the dependency
range.

### Changes
- **`sdk/core/core-xml/package.json`**: Raise minimum `fast-xml-parser`
from `^5.0.7` → `^5.3.7`
- **`sdk/core/core-xml/CHANGELOG.md`**: Document security fix in
unreleased `1.5.1` section

### Packages impacted by this PR
`@azure/core-xml`

### Issues associated with this PR
-
[GHSA-jmr7-xgp7-cmfj](https://github.com/advisories/GHSA-jmr7-xgp7-cmfj)
— DoS via unbounded entity expansion in DOCTYPE
-
[GHSA-m7jm-9gc2-mpf2](https://github.com/advisories/GHSA-m7jm-9gc2-mpf2)
— Entity encoding bypass via regex injection in DOCTYPE entity names

### Describe the problem that is addressed by this PR
`fast-xml-parser` versions 4.1.3–5.3.5 have two critical
vulnerabilities. The floating `^5.0.7` range allows any vulnerable 5.x
version to be resolved. Raising the floor to `^5.3.7` ensures only
patched versions are used.

### What are the possible designs available to address the problem? If
there are more than one possible design, why was the one in this PR
chosen?
Only option: update the minimum version constraint. No API changes
required; `fast-xml-parser` 5.3.7 is backwards-compatible.

### Are there test cases added in this PR? _(If not, why?)_
No. This is a dependency version floor bump with no behavioral changes —
existing tests provide sufficient coverage.

### Provide a list of related PRs _(if any)_
Prior art: [1.3.4 bump of fast-xml-parser to
`^4.2.4`](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-js/blob/main/sdk/core/core-xml/CHANGELOG.md#134-2023-06-20)
for a prior security issue.

### Checklists

create an Issue in the
[Autorest/typescript](https://github.com/Azure/autorest.typescript)
repository and link it here.)_

OpenTelemetry] Add Support for Custom Loggers","body":"### Packages impacted by this PR
@azure/monitor-opentelemetry

### Issues associated with this PR
https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-js/issues/33975

### Describe the problem that is addressed by this PR
This pull request introduces support for providing a custom diagnostic logger in the Azure Monitor OpenTelemetry SDK, allowing consumers to route OpenTelemetry diagnostics and Azure SDK logging through their own logging frameworks. The implementation ensures that the custom logger is used consistently throughout the SDK and maintains backward compatibility with the default logger. Comprehensive unit tests have been added to verify the new behavior and edge cases.

Custom diagnostic logger support:

* Added a `diagnosticLogger` option to the `AzureMonitorOpenTelemetryOptions` interface, enabling consumers to supply a custom `DiagLogger` for OpenTelemetry diagnostics and Azure SDK logging. [[1]](diffhunk://#diff-[REDACTED]R21) [[2]](diffhunk://#diff-[REDACTED]R43-R49) [[3]](diffhunk://#diff-[REDACTED]R9)
* Modified the `Logger` singleton to accept an optional custom logger, and updated its initialization logic to use the provided logger for OpenTelemetry and Azure SDK logs, falling back to the default `DiagFileConsoleLogger` when not supplied. [[1]](diffhunk://#diff-[REDACTED]L18-R25) [[2]](diffhunk://#diff-[REDACTED]R58-R61) [[3]](diffhunk://#diff-[REDACTED]L86-R92)
* Updated the `useAzureMonitor` function to initialize logging with the custom logger if provided in options, ensuring early adoption of custom logging.

Testing and documentation:

* Added detailed unit tests in `logger.test.ts` to validate custom logger usage, singleton behavior, routing of AzureLogger logs, log level handling, and robustness against undefined logger inputs.
* Updated API review and documentation files to reflect the new `diagnosticLogger` option and its intended behavior. [[1]](diffhunk://#diff-[REDACTED]R8) [[2]](diffhunk://#diff-[REDACTED]R4)

### Checklists
- [x] Added impacted package name to the issue description
- [ ] Does this PR needs any fixes in the SDK Generator?** _(If so, create an Issue in the [Autorest/typescript](https://github.com/Azure/autorest.typescript) repository and link it here)_
- [x] Added a changelog (if necessary)

add a tool to report js repo health status","body":"following what python team has at
https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-python/tree/main/scripts/repo_health_status_report

This tool gathers latest azure pipeline build results from Azure DevOps customer-reported issues from GitHub and then associates them with their corresponding packages. The results are saved in a CSV file which is then uploaded to GitHub `Azure/azure-sdk-for-js` repository in the `js-sdk-health-report` branch.

A pipeline definition is also added to run the tool.

rollup dependency to 4.53.1","body":"### Packages impacted by this PR

- @azure/ai-form-recognizer
- @azure/batch
- @azure/api-management-custom-widgets-scaffolder

### Issues associated with this PR

Resolves dependency update issue for rollup package.

### Describe the problem that is addressed by this PR

Three packages were pinned to outdated rollup versions (^4.0.0 and ^4.14.0), preventing them from receiving bug fixes and performance improvements available in 4.53.x.

### What are the possible designs available to address the problem? If there are more than one possible design, why was the one in this PR chosen?

Direct version bump to ^4.53.1 using caret range. This allows automatic patch upgrades while maintaining compatibility. Version 4.53.2 exists but has pnpm registry propagation issues; the caret range will auto-upgrade once resolved.

### Are there test cases added in this PR? _(If not, why?)_

No. This is a dependency version update with no breaking changes (versions 4.53.0-4.53.1 contain only bug fixes and performance improvements). Rollup functionality verified post-install.

### Provide a list of related PRs _(if any)_

N/A

### Command used to generate this PR:**_(Applicable only to SDK release request PRs)_

N/A

### Checklists
- [x] Added impacted package name to the issue description
- [x] Does this PR needs any fixes in the SDK Generator?** _(If so, create an Issue in the [Autorest/typescript](https://github.com/Azure/autorest.typescript) repository and link it here)_
- [ ] Added a changelog (if necessary)







Original prompt

> 
> ----
> 
> *This section details on the original issue you should resolve*
> 
> Dependency package rollup has a new version available
> We have identified a dependency on version 4.53.1 of [rollup](https://www.npmjs.com/package/rollup). A new version (4.53.2) is available for upgrade.
> 
> Following are the steps to upgrade package dependency.
> 
>   1. Understand the breaking changes between the version being used and the version you want to upgrade to.
> 
>   2. Identify all packages that take a dependency on this package.
> 
>   3. Go to the root folder for each such package (/sdk/service-name/package-name) and update package.json to have the new version.
> 
>   4. Run 'pnpm install' to ensure the new version is pulled in.
> 
>   5. Make relevant changes to absorb the breaking changes.
> 
>   6. Repeat steps 3 to 5 for each of the packages that have a dependency on this package.
> 
> ## Comments on the Issue (you are @copilot in this section)
> 
> 
> 
> 




- Fixes Azure/azure-sdk-for-js#36524

certificate operation cancellation race condition with retry logic","body":"The Key Vault Certificates nightly tests were failing with HTTP 409 conflict errors when attempting to cancel certificate operations immediately after creation. The error message indicated:

> There was a conflict while updating pending certificate... This can happen when multiple operations are performed in parallel against the pending certificate. To avoid this error, perform operations against the pending certificate in a sequential manner.

## Root Cause

The issue was a race condition in the test flow:
1. Test creates a certificate operation (`beginCreateCertificate`)
2. Immediately retrieves the operation (`getCertificateOperation`) 
3. Immediately tries to cancel it (`cancelOperation`)

The Key Vault service hadn't finished processing the initial creation request when the cancellation request arrived, resulting in HTTP 409 conflicts.

## Solution

Added retry logic specifically for HTTP 409 conflict errors in the `cancelCertificateOperation` method:

- **Retries up to 3 times** on HTTP 409 conflict errors only
- **Uses exponential backoff**: 500ms, 1000ms, 2000ms delays between retries
- **Non-conflict errors are immediately rethrown** - no unnecessary retries
- **Leverages existing Azure SDK utilities**: `delay` from `@azure/core-util` and `isRestError` from `@azure/core-rest-pipeline`

## Testing

Added comprehensive unit tests to verify:
- Retry behavior on 409 conflict errors succeeds after retry
- Non-409 errors are not retried and fail immediately  
- Max retry limit is respected for persistent conflicts

The original failing test now passes consistently, completing in ~92ms instead of timing out.

Fixes #35011.

Add check to prevent workspace specifiers in runtime dependencies","body":"### Packages impacted by this PR

- `@azure/dev-tool`

### Issues associated with this PR

Addresses the issue where packages can be released with `workspace:` specifiers in runtime dependencies, which resolve to unpublished versions since we increment package versions after release.

- Fixes Azure/azure-sdk-for-js#36580

### Describe the problem that is addressed by this PR

pnpm `workspace:` specifiers in the `dependencies` field of non-private packages can reference unpublished versions, breaking installation for consumers when the package is released to npm.

### What are the possible designs available to address the problem? If there are more than one possible design, why was the one in this PR chosen?

Added a packageJson check tagged with `"release"` that runs during pre-release validation. The check:
- Fails for non-private packages with `workspace:` in `dependencies`
- Exempts private packages (not published)
- Allows `workspace:` in `devDependencies` (not runtime deps)

Alternative approaches like pre-publish hooks or CI-only checks would miss local validation and increase iteration time.

### Are there test cases added in this PR? _(If not, why?)_

Yes. Added 5 test cases covering:
- Non-private packages with/without workspace specifiers
- Private package exemption
- devDependencies handling
- Empty dependencies edge case

Also fixed missing `await` in `packageJsonCheck` framework that caused unhandled promise rejections.

### Provide a list of related PRs _(if any)_

N/A

### Command used to generate this PR:**_(Applicable only to SDK release request PRs)_

N/A

### Checklists
- [x] Added impacted package name to the issue description
- [x] Does this PR needs any fixes in the SDK Generator?** _(If so, create an Issue in the [Autorest/typescript](https://github.com/Azure/autorest.typescript) repository and link it here)_
- [x] Added a changelog (if necessary)







Original prompt

> 
> ----
> 
> *This section details on the original issue you should resolve*
> 
> [dev-tool] add a check to ensure that a package's runtime deps doesn't use any pnpm workspace specifier
> Today because we increment package versions after release, the `workspace:` specifier most of time resolves to an unpublished version.  This causes problem if we release a package with unpublished dependencies.
> 
> This item tracks adding a check to `common/tools/dev-tool/` check command/framework that verifies that a non-private package's `dependencies` list doesn't use any `workspace:` specifiers
> 
> ## Comments on the Issue (you are @copilot in this section)
> 
> 
> 
> 




- Fixes Azure/azure-sdk-for-js#36580

typo: agentDefintion → agentDefinition in v2 agent samples","body":"### Packages impacted by this PR

`@azure/ai-projects`

### Issues associated with this PR

Addresses feedback from #36577

### Describe the problem that is addressed by this PR

Variable name typo `agentDefintion` (missing 'i') existed in 4 agent tool sample files, affecting code readability and potentially causing confusion for developers using these samples as reference.

### What are the possible designs available to address the problem? If there are more than one possible design, why was the one in this PR chosen?

Direct string replacement of the misspelled variable name. No design alternatives needed for a typo fix.

### Are there test cases added in this PR? _(If not, why?)_

No. This is a variable name correction with no functional changes. Existing samples remain functionally identical.

### Provide a list of related PRs _(if any)_

- #36577 (parent PR this stacks on)

### Command used to generate this PR:**_(Applicable only to SDK release request PRs)_

N/A

### Checklists
- [x] Added impacted package name to the issue description
- [ ] Does this PR needs any fixes in the SDK Generator?** _(If so, create an Issue in the [Autorest/typescript](https://github.com/Azure/autorest.typescript) repository and link it here)_
- [ ] Added a changelog (if necessary)

typo: agentDefintion → agentDefinition in v2 sample files","body":"### Packages impacted by this PR

`@azure-samples/ai-projects-ts-beta`

### Issues associated with this PR

Addresses feedback from #36577

### Describe the problem that is addressed by this PR

Variable name typo `agentDefintion` instead of `agentDefinition` in multiple v2 TypeScript sample files.

### What are the possible designs available to address the problem? If there are more than one possible design, why was the one in this PR chosen?

Straightforward typo correction. Fixed both declaration and usage in all affected files:
- `agentBingCustomSearch.ts`
- `agentAgentToAgent.ts`
- `agentSharepoint.ts`
- `agentBrowserAutomation.ts`

### Are there test cases added in this PR? _(If not, why?)_

No - typo fix only, no behavioral changes.

### Provide a list of related PRs _(if any)_

- Parent PR: #36577
- Original feedback: https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-js/pull/36577#discussion_r2524367006

### Command used to generate this PR:**_(Applicable only to SDK release request PRs)_

N/A

### Checklists
- [x] Added impacted package name to the issue description
- [ ] Does this PR needs any fixes in the SDK Generator?** _(If so, create an Issue in the [Autorest/typescript](https://github.com/Azure/autorest.typescript) repository and link it here)_
- [ ] Added a changelog (if necessary)

typo: agentDefintion → agentDefinition in v2-beta samples","body":"### Packages impacted by this PR

- @azure/ai-projects

### Issues associated with this PR

Related to #36577

### Describe the problem that is addressed by this PR

Variable name typo `agentDefintion` instead of `agentDefinition` in v2-beta sample files for Agent to Agent and Browser Automation tools.

### What are the possible designs available to address the problem? If there are more than one possible design, why was the one in this PR chosen?

Rename the variable to the correct spelling across all affected files.

### Are there test cases added in this PR? _(If not, why?)_

No. This is a variable name correction in sample code with no behavioral changes.

### Provide a list of related PRs _(if any)_

- #36577 (parent PR)

### Command used to generate this PR:**_(Applicable only to SDK release request PRs)_

N/A

- [x] Added impacted package name to the issue description

typo in variable name: agentDefintion → agentDefinition","body":"### Packages impacted by this PR

@azure/ai-projects

### Issues associated with this PR

Addresses feedback from #36577 (comment discussion #2524529983)

### Describe the problem that is addressed by this PR

Variable name typo "agentDefintion" appeared across multiple agent tool samples in both JavaScript and TypeScript.

### What are the possible designs available to address the problem? If there are more than one possible design, why was the one in this PR chosen?

Single design: correct the spelling to "agentDefinition" throughout.

### Are there test cases added in this PR? _(If not, why?)_

No - this is a variable name correction in sample code.

### Provide a list of related PRs _(if any)_

- Parent PR: #36577

### Command used to generate this PR:**_(Applicable only to SDK release request PRs)_

N/A

custom agent for package deprecation","body":"### Packages impacted by this PR

N/A - Infrastructure change

### Issues associated with this PR

#36551 (example issue referenced in problem statement)

### Describe the problem that is addressed by this PR

Package deprecation requires three manual steps: removing the package directory, updating `.github/CODEOWNERS`, and running `pnpm install --no-frozen-lockfile` to update lockfile. This is repetitive and error-prone.

### What are the possible designs available to address the problem? If there are more than one possible design, why was the one in this PR chosen?

Custom GitHub Copilot agent with specialized prompts provides automated, guided deprecation workflow. Alternative would be a script, but agent approach:
- Validates each step interactively
- Handles edge cases with human oversight
- Self-documents the process
- Integrates with existing Copilot workflow

### Are there test cases added in this PR? _(If not, why?)_

No. This adds agent configuration/documentation. Agent behavior will be validated through usage.

### Provide a list of related PRs _(if any)_

#36591 - Example PR showing the pattern this agent automates

### Command used to generate this PR:**_(Applicable only to SDK release request PRs)_

N/A

### Checklists
- [x] Added impacted package name to the issue description
- [x] Does this PR needs any fixes in the SDK Generator?** _(If so, create an Issue in the [Autorest/typescript](https://github.com/Azure/autorest.typescript) repository and link it here)_
- [x] Added a changelog (if necessary)

---

## Changes

### New File

**`.github/agents/deprecate-package.agent.md`** - Custom agent definition (107 lines)
- Concise workflow: verify → remove directory → update CODEOWNERS → run `pnpm install --no-frozen-lockfile`
- Validation steps at each stage
- Error handling for missing packages/entries
- Explicit scope limitations (does not handle npm deprecation notices, CI/CD changes, etc.)
- Uses portable placeholder `{full path to repository root}` instead of hardcoded paths
- Clarified CODEOWNERS comment removal guidance (only remove comments unique to the package)
- Proper verification commands that correctly check for removal success
- Uses `.agent.md` extension per GitHub's custom agent documentation

### Directory Structure

Following [GitHub's official documentation](https://docs.github.com/en/copilot/how-tos/use-copilot-agents/coding-agent/create-custom-agents), the new custom agent is placed in `.github/agents/` directory with the `.agent.md` file extension. The existing `test-recording.prompt.md` remains in its original location at `.github/prompts/`.

### Agent Workflow Example

```bash
# Given: @azure/arm-hdinsightcontainers deprecation request

# 1. Remove package
cd {full path to repository root}
rm -rf sdk/hdinsight/arm-hdinsightcontainers/

# 2. Update CODEOWNERS (remove entry and comment only if unique to this package)
# /sdk/hdinsight/arm-hdinsightcontainers/ @qiaozha @MaryGao

# 3. Update lockfile
pnpm install --no-frozen-lockfile
```

Agent follows the `mode: "agent"` frontmatter pattern as specified in GitHub's custom agent documentation.







Original prompt

> We only receive request to deprecate a package. an example is this issue: https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-js/issues/36551.  To accomplish that we need to remove the directory of that package, remove its entry in .github/CODEOWNERS, and run `pnpm install` to update the pnpm-lock.yaml file.  An example pull request is https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-js/pull/36591.  Help me create a custom copilot agent who is an expert in doing this and nothing else.

Fix memory leak in IdentityClient abort controller management","body":"### Packages impacted by this PR
- `@azure/identity`

### Issues associated with this PR
Memory leak in long-lived IdentityClient instances due to unbounded growth of AbortController map.

### Describe the problem that is addressed by this PR
`IdentityClient` creates and stores `AbortController` instances in a map for each HTTP request but only cleaned them up when abort was triggered. Controllers from normally-completing requests (success or failure) accumulated indefinitely.

### What are the possible designs available to address the problem? If there are more than one possible design, why was the one in this PR chosen?
**Chosen approach:** Add cleanup in `finally` blocks of request methods to guarantee removal regardless of outcome.

**Alternatives considered:**
- Weak references: Not available in all target environments
- TTL-based cleanup: Adds complexity and timer overhead
- Size-based eviction: Arbitrary limits, doesn't prevent leaks

### Are there test cases added in this PR? _(If not, why?)_
Yes. Tests verify `Map` entries are deleted after:
- Successful GET/POST requests  
- Failed requests (using 400 status code to avoid retry timeouts)
- Requests with different correlation IDs

### Provide a list of related PRs _(if any)_
None

### Command used to generate this PR:**_(Applicable only to SDK release request PRs)_
N/A

### Checklists
- [x] Added impacted package name to the issue description
- [ ] Does this PR needs any fixes in the SDK Generator?** _(If so, create an Issue in the [Autorest/typescript](https://github.com/Azure/autorest.typescript) repository and link it here)_
- [ ] Added a changelog (if necessary)

---

## Implementation

**Changed methods:**
- `cleanupAbortControllers()`: New private method using `Map.delete()` to remove entries
- `sendGetRequestAsync()`: Wrapped in try-finally with cleanup
- `sendPostRequestAsync()`: Wrapped in try-finally with cleanup  
- `generateAbortSignal()`: Changed from `set(id, undefined)` to `delete(id)`
- `abortRequests()`: Changed from `set(id, undefined)` to `delete(id)`

**Code example:**
```typescript
async sendGetRequestAsync(url: string, options?: NetworkRequestOptions): Promise> {
  const correlationId = noCorrelationId;
  const request = createPipelineRequest({
    url,
    method: "GET",
    abortSignal: this.generateAbortSignal(correlationId),
    // ...
  });

  try {
    const response = await this.sendRequest(request);
    return { body: this.parseResponseBody(response) as T, /* ... */ };
  } finally {
    // Prevents leak by removing map entry after request completes
    this.cleanupAbortControllers(correlationId);
  }
}
```

## Test Implementation Note

The error handling test uses a 400 status code instead of 500 to avoid triggering ServiceClient's retry logic (maxRetries: 3), which would cause test timeouts. A 400 status is sufficient to verify cleanup occurs for non-successful responses.







Original prompt

> 
> ----
> 
> *This section details on the original issue you should resolve*
> 
> [identity] potential memory leak if IdentityClient is kept long-lived
> We have code in identityClient that creates and adds abortControllers to a map of ``. It seems to always add abort controller regardless of whether there's an abortSignal passed in. It also only clears the array when abort signal is aborted.
> 
> https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-js/blob/[REDACTED]/sdk/identity/identity/src/client/identityClient.ts#L204-L233
> 
> We should also clean up when abort signal is aborted, that is, when request completes
>

### Packages impacted by this PR

None (infrastructure/tooling only)

### Issues associated with this PR

Adds `.github/lsp.json` so GitHub Copilot CLI can use
`typescript-language-server` for code intelligence in this repo.

### Describe the problem that is addressed by this PR

Copilot CLI supports [repository-level LSP
configuration](https://github.com/github/copilot-cli?tab=readme-ov-file#configuring-lsp-servers)
to enable go-to-definition, hover, and diagnostics. This repo had no
such configuration.

### What are the possible designs available to address the problem? If
there are more than one possible design, why was the one in this PR
chosen?

Single file at `.github/lsp.json` per the Copilot CLI docs. Maps `.ts`,
`.tsx`, `.mts`, and `.cts` to `typescript-language-server --stdio`.

### Are there test cases added in this PR? _(If not, why?)_

No — this is a static JSON configuration file consumed by external
tooling.

### Provide a list of related PRs _(if any)_

None

- [x] Added impacted package name to the issue description.
- [ ] Does this PR need any fixes in the SDK Generator?** _(If so,
create an Issue in the
[Autorest/typescript](https://github.com/Azure/autorest.typescript)
repository and link it here.)_
- [ ] Added a changelog (if necessary).

### Packages impacted by this PR

`eng/tools/analyze-deps`, `eng/tools/generate-doc`,
`eng/tools/js-sdk-release-tools`

### Issues associated with this PR


### Describe the problem that is addressed by this PR

Runs `npm audit fix` across all npm-managed packages under `eng/tools`
to resolve known dependency vulnerabilities.

**Fixed (lock file updates only):**
- `analyze-deps`: `tar` 7.5.4 → 7.5.11 (path traversal via
hardlink/symlink)
- `generate-doc`: `markdown-it` 14.1.0 → 14.1.1 (ReDoS), `minimatch`
9.0.5 → 9.0.9 (ReDoS)
- `js-sdk-release-tools`: `flatted` 3.3.4 → 3.4.1 (unbounded recursion
DoS), `tar` 7.5.9 → 7.5.11 (path traversal)

**No vulnerabilities found:** `eng-package-utils`, `versioning`

**Unfixable without breaking change:** `ajv` ReDoS in
`js-sdk-release-tools` — transitive via `@typespec/compiler`, fix
requires upstream update.

### What are the possible designs available to address the problem? If
there are more than one possible design, why was the one in this PR
chosen?

`npm audit fix` (non-breaking semver-compatible updates) vs `npm audit
fix --force` (allows breaking changes). Used non-force to avoid
downgrading `@azure-tools/js-sdk-release-tools` from 2.16.4 to 2.7.23.

### Are there test cases added in this PR? _(If not, why?)_

No — changes are limited to `package-lock.json` files (dependency
version bumps within existing semver ranges).

### Packages impacted by this PR

ci-runner

### Describe the problem that is addressed by this PR

Add a new ci-runner action that verifies packages with already-published
versions have no source modifications since the release tag. Fails if a
published version has modified files, indicating the version needs to be
bumped.

### What are the possible designs available to address the problem? If
there are more than one possible design, why was the one in this PR
chosen?

Currently we solve this by automatically opening version bump PRs after
release. However, these PRs do not always get merged in a timely fashion
and generate considerable noise.

### Are there test cases added in this PR? _(If not, why?)_

Yes

https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-js/pull/36022

**Background**
All management SDKs are auto generated and their PR reviewings are
mainly rule-based. The internal implementation seems un-necessary and
direct suggested changes are wrong guidances. So we build the rule-based
review process in agentica workflow to highlight checkpoints mgmt
reviewers care and provide correct suggestions when issue happens.

**How to trigger?**
Adding the label `mgmt-review-needed` would automatcially trigger our
rule-based reviews.

**Detail**
Add management SDK review agent and agentic workflow that triggers when
the 'mgmt-review-needed' label is added to a PR. The reviews for public
API design issues including breaking changes, naming conventions and API
consistency and tool validation rules to detect any tool issues during
preparing AutoPRs.

This is the first version and we would continue improving its quality
https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-js/issues/37631.

**Testing PRs**
- https://github.com/MaryGao/azure-sdk-for-js/pull/17
- https://github.com/MaryGao/azure-sdk-for-js/pull/18
- https://github.com/MaryGao/azure-sdk-for-js/pull/19

Co-authored-by: kazrael2119 <98569699+kazrael2119@users.noreply.github.com>","author":{"name":"Azure SDK Bot","email":"53356347+azure-sdk@users.noreply.github.com","date":"2026-03-17T16:07:15Z"},"committer":{"name":"GitHub","email":"noreply@github.com","date":"2026-03-17T16:07:15Z"}},"author":{"login":"azure-sdk","id":53356347,"profile_url":"https://github.com/azure-sdk","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/53356347?v=4"},"committer":{"login":"web-flow","id":19864447,"profile_url":"https://github.com/web-flow","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/19864447?v=4"}},{"sha":"[REDACTED]","html_url":"https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-js/commit/[REDACTED]","commit":{"message":"[search] Add agent skill for search-documents package (#37484)

This adds an [agent skill](https://agentskills.io/specification) for the
search-documents package - encoding the package-specific tribal
knowledge that an AI agent needs when working on this library.

### Motivation

Working on `@azure/search-documents` has a lot of package-specific
gotchas: the custom pagination implementation, the
`additionalProperties` unwrapping pattern, the type conversion layer in
`serviceUtils.ts`, pipe-delimited semantic encoding, the known skills
whitelist that silently drops unknown types, etc. This skill encodes
that knowledge so an agent can load it on demand and avoid the usual
iteration cycles.

### What's here

**Registry skill** (`.github/skills/find-package-skill/SKILL.md`)
- Discovered automatically by agents from the repo root
- Routes to the correct package-specific skill based on what package is
being modified
- Instructs the agent to read the SKILL.md and any `references/` files

**Package skill**
(`sdk/search/search-documents/.github/skills/search-documents/`)
- `SKILL.md` - the main skill covering architecture, regeneration, the
four clients, custom pagination, data flow patterns
(additionalProperties unwrapping, serialization sandwich, field
visibility inversion), wire format encodings, type mappings, and common
pitfalls
- `references/customization.md` - the 3-way merge algorithm and common
post-regeneration scenarios
- `references/type-mapping.md` - property name changes and conversion
function inventory

### Conventions

I wanted to set up a pattern that other packages could follow:
- **Encapsulation**: skill lives alongside the package it describes
(`sdk/<service>/<package>/.github/skills/<name>/`)
- **Registry**: `find-package-skill` at the repo root provides the entry
point - package skills don't need to be in a global scan path
- **Scope**: only package-specific tribal knowledge, not generic repo or
SDK guidance (build commands, test framework, etc. belong in
`AGENTS.md`)
- **References**: deeper detail in `references/` directory for
progressive disclosure","author":{"name":"Maor Leger","email":"maorleger@users.noreply.github.com","date":"2026-03-17T15:42:43Z"},"committer":{"name":"GitHub","email":"noreply@github.com","date":"2026-03-17T15:42:43Z"}},"author":{"login":"maorleger","id":753570,"profile_url":"https://github.com/maorleger","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/753570?v=4"},"committer":{"login":"web-flow","id":19864447,"profile_url":"https://github.com/web-flow","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/19864447?v=4"}},{"sha":"[REDACTED]","html_url":"https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-js/commit/[REDACTED]","commit":{"message":"chore(loadtesting):added sourceType un wsEndpoint (#37672)

stable releases and regenerates the lock file.

### Describe the problem that is addressed by this PR

TypeSpec emitter dependencies in `eng/emitter-package.json` were pinned
to outdated versions. This PR bumps them to latest stable and
regenerates the lock file.

**`eng/emitter-package.json` version changes:**
- `@typespec/{events,rest,streams,versioning}`: `0.79.0` → `0.80.0`
- `@typespec/{openapi,openapi3}`: `1.9.0` → `1.10.0`
- `@typespec/http`: `1.9.1` → `1.10.0`
- `@azure-tools/typespec-ts`: `0.50.4` → `0.51.0`
-
`@azure-tools/typespec-{azure-core,autorest,azure-resource-manager,azure-rulesets}`:
`0.65.0` → `0.66.0`
- `@azure-tools/typespec-client-generator-core`: `0.65.4` → `0.66.2`
- `@azure-tools/typespec-liftr-base`: `0.12.0` → `0.13.0`

**`.scripts/automation_generate.sh`:**
- `@autorest/typescript`: `^6.0.63` → `^6.0.64`

**`eng/emitter-package-lock.json`:** Regenerated via `tsp-client
generate-lock-file`.

### Packages impacted by this PR
@azure/monitor-opentelemetry-exporter

### Issues associated with this PR

https://github.com/microsoft/ApplicationInsights-node.js/issues/317#issuecomment-3336028029

### Describe the problem that is addressed by this PR
This pull request introduces improvements to the Azure Monitor
OpenTelemetry Exporter, focusing on handling throttled telemetry and
respecting backend retry guidance. The exporter now persists throttled
telemetry for retry, and uses the `Retry-After` header to schedule
retries more accurately. Several tests and utility functions were added
to ensure robust handling of retry scenarios.

**Telemetry retry and persistence enhancements:**

* Throttled telemetry (HTTP 429/439 responses) is now persisted to disk
for retry instead of being silently dropped, improving reliability for
transient backend throttling.
[[1]](diffhunk://#diff-[REDACTED]R3-R9)
[[2]](diffhunk://#diff-[REDACTED]L157-R152)
* The exporter now respects the `Retry-After` header from the backend
when scheduling retries for both throttled and successful telemetry,
using a new `scheduleRetryTimer` method.
[[1]](diffhunk://#diff-[REDACTED]L130-R137)
[[2]](diffhunk://#diff-[REDACTED]R397-R407)
[[3]](diffhunk://#diff-[REDACTED]L76-R81)

**Utility and type improvements:**

* Added the `parseRetryAfterHeader` utility function to robustly parse
both delay-seconds and HTTP-date formats from the `Retry-After` header.
* Updated the `SenderResult` type to include an optional `retryAfterMs`
property, enabling downstream logic to use backend-provided retry
intervals.

**Testing enhancements:**

* Added and extended tests to verify envelope persistence and retry
scheduling on throttled responses, as well as correct parsing and usage
of the `Retry-After` header.
[[1]](diffhunk://#diff-[REDACTED]R654-R705)
[[2]](diffhunk://#diff-[REDACTED]R1-R55)

{"total_count":7,"incomplete_results":false,"items":[{"id":4063650917,"number":37612,"state":"open","locked":false,"title":"CI test failures on main: `@azure/attestation`","body":"## Summary

1 package is affected by live test failures detected in CI on the `main` branch. The HEAD commit `[REDACTED]` (merged 2026-03-12) shows 2 failing check runs, both in the `js - attestation - tests` CI job. 1 test is failing out of 212 total. The failure is in a live-mode test (the attestation package runs against real Azure Attestation infrastructure, not in playback mode).

## Failures

### `@azure/attestation`

**Failing tests:**
- `[AAD] Attestation Client > #attestTpm` in `sdk/attestation/attestation/test/public/attestationTests.spec.ts`

**Error:**

```
A VBS attestation policy has not been set on the attestation provider.
```

Reported in check run `js - attestation - tests` (id: `66769152361`) and `js - attestation - tests (Public windows_20x_browser)` (id: `66768403727`) on commit `[REDACTED]`.

**Root cause:**

The `#attestTpm` test is a **live** test (as configured in `sdk/attestation/attestation/tests.yml` via the `archetype-sdk-tests.yml` pipeline template and `platform-matrix.json`). It runs against a real Azure Attestation service instance rather than using recorded HTTP playback.

The test first sets a TPM attestation policy on the service, then calls `client.attestTpm()`. The service is returning an error indicating "A VBS attestation policy has not been set on the attestation provider." This is a service-side infrastructure issue: the Azure Attestation provider instance used by CI does not have a VBS (Virtualization Based Security) attestation policy configured, which the service now requires before it will process TPM attestation requests.

The test code itself is:
```typescript
it("#attestTpm", async () => {
  const client = createRecordedClient(recorder, "AAD", true);
  const adminClient = createRecordedAdminClient(recorder, "AAD");

  // Set the policy on the instance to a known value.
  await adminClient.setPolicy(
    KnownAttestationType.Tpm,
    "version=1.0; authorizationrules{=> permit();}; issuancerules{};",
  );

  const encodedPayload = JSON.stringify({ payload: { type: "aikcert" } });
  const result = await client.attestTpm(encodedPayload);
  // ...
});
```

The test sets a `Tpm` policy successfully but the subsequent `attestTpm` call fails with the VBS policy error. This indicates a change in the Azure Attestation service behaviour: a VBS policy must now also be configured on the provider before TPM attestation can proceed.

**Suggested fix:**

This is **not a code bug** — it is a live-test infrastructure configuration issue. No source code change is needed. The fix requires one of the following:

1. **Configure VBS policy on the CI attestation provider**: The CI service principal that manages the attestation provider needs to set a VBS attestation policy (e.g., `version=1.0; authorizationrules{=> permit();}; issuancerules{};`) on the `Vbs` attestation type before the TPM test can succeed. This is done through the Azure portal or via `az attestation policy set`.

2. **Update the test to also set a VBS policy**: If VBS policy is now always required alongside TPM policy for this test scenario, update the test to also set (and then reset) a VBS policy before calling `attestTpm`:

```diff
--- a/sdk/attestation/attestation/test/public/attestationTests.spec.ts
+++ b/sdk/attestation/attestation/test/public/attestationTests.spec.ts
@@ -169,6 +169,10 @@ describe("[AAD] Attestation Client", () => {
     await adminClient.setPolicy(
       KnownAttestationType.Tpm,
       "version=1.0; authorizationrules{=> permit();}; issuancerules{};",
     );
+    await adminClient.setPolicy(
+      KnownAttestationType.Vbs,
+      "version=1.0; authorizationrules{=> permit();}; issuancerules{};",
+    );
 
     const encodedPayload = JSON.stringify({ payload: { type: "aikcert" } });
 
@@ -182,6 +186,7 @@ describe("[AAD] Attestation Client", () => {
 
     // Reset the policy on the instance to the default for reproducibility.
     await adminClient.resetPolicy(KnownAttestationType.Tpm);
+    await adminClient.resetPolicy(KnownAttestationType.Vbs);
   });
```

Note: Before adopting option 2, verify whether `KnownAttestationType.Vbs` exists in the SDK or needs to be added, and confirm with the attestation service team that VBS policy is now a prerequisite for TPM attestation. Option 1 (infrastructure fix) is preferred if the test environment can be updated.

**Reproduced locally:** No (live test — requires Azure Attestation service credentials and infrastructure with TPM hardware support)

---

## Additional Notes

- The attestation test suite runs exclusively in **live mode** against real Azure Attestation infrastructure (see `sdk/attestation/attestation/tests.yml`). There are recorded playback files for this test (`recording_attesttpm.json` in `Azure/azure-sdk-assets` tag `js/attestation/attestation_bc4c12556e`) that show all 4 requests returning HTTP 200, meaning a previous recording was successful. The current failure is due to a service-side behaviour change in the live environment.
- The HEAD commit (`[REDACTED]`) was an AutoPR for `@azure-arm-postgresql-flexible` — it has no relation to the attestation package. This confirms the failure is an **environmental/service configuration regression**, not a code regression from the merged PR.
- The browser test check run (`js - attestation - tests (Public windows_20x_browser)`) shows only a generic `PowerShell exited with code '1'` failure with no additional test-level annotation, suggesting it was aborted or hit the same service error.
